algNote: Informational. The live door takes `alg` from the trusted mandate's terminal block (pop.ts), NEVER from the caller or this vector.

testKey.role: TEST-ONLY — trusted by NO door. The door binds the terminal key to a trusted mandate root; this key fails that bind. Published only for KAT reproducibility.

signedPayload.description: The signature is over canonicalize({jti, ts, capabilityId, workspaceId, agentId, input}). The door RECONSTRUCTS this from the request + proof; it is NOT transmitted as one object.

transmitted.description: handleCall(req, proof). `proof` carries ONLY {jti, ts, sig}; the other 4 signed fields live on `req` and are re-derived door-side.

canonicalization.summary: Signed bytes = UTF-8(JSON.stringify(stableSort(payload))). Reproduce WITHOUT a JS engine using the rules below.
canonicalization.keySort: Recursively sort object keys ascending by UTF-16 code unit (JavaScript String comparison). Arrays keep insertion order (NOT sorted). Drop keys whose value is undefined or a function.
canonicalization.numbers: ECMAScript Number::toString (IEEE-754 double, shortest round-trip): no trailing '.0' on integers, '-0' serializes as '0', large/small magnitudes use exponent form ('1e+21'). Integers above 2^53-1 are unsafe (lossy) — do not rely on them.
canonicalization.strings: Raw UTF-8 bytes. Non-ASCII is NOT \uXXXX-escaped. Escaped per JSON: the control set U+0000–U+001F, plus '"' and '\\'. U+2028/U+2029 are emitted literally by JSON.stringify (NOT escaped) — match that.
canonicalization.encoding: UTF-8 of the resulting JSON text. The signature is over those bytes (see canonicalBytesHex), not the string.

freshness.description: A byte-perfect signature is necessary but not sufficient; the door also enforces:
freshness.rule: Accept iff (now - ts) <= 60 AND (ts - now) <= 5. `jti` is single-use within window+skew (65s). Vectors use a FIXED past `ts`, so they are signature KATs — a LIVE call MUST stamp `ts` near now and a fresh `jti`.

precedence.note: verifyPoP order (pop.ts): freshness -> mandate trust-bind -> terminal-key identity bind (terminalKey===credential.agentPublicKey, workspaceId/agentId match) -> SIGNATURE -> replay. A correct signature is checked only AFTER the credential is trust-bound; this vector covers the signature step only.

vectors.name: numbers-float-neg-exp
vectors.note: Exercises JS Number::toString: float, -0 -> '0', exponent, integer at the 2^53 safe edge. Integers > 2^53 are NOT representable (JS Number); producers MUST treat numbers with JS-Number/IEEE-754 shortest-round-trip semantics.

vectors.name: nested-out-of-order
vectors.note: Nested objects with reverse-sorted keys + arrays. Verifies recursion (nested keys sorted) AND array order preserved (NOT sorted).

vectors.name: unicode-surrogate
vectors.note: Non-ASCII keys+values + an astral-plane emoji (surrogate pair). Verifies raw UTF-8 encoding (NOT \uXXXX escaping) and UTF-16-code-unit key sort.
